summary: Russian operators of the "Rubikon" FPV drone unit reported 110 targets eliminated on March 18, 2026, across multiple sectors including Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman, with operations targeting Ukrainian personnel and fixed-wing UAVs. The "Elka" kinetic anti-drone system intercepted another enemy fixed-wing drone in the special military operation zone. On the Zaporizhzhia front, the "Dnipro" volunteer battalion's FPV air defense crew downed 9 Domaha, 4 Darts, 3 Chaklun, 2 Baton, 2 Maya, 2 Fury, 2 Vector, 2 Mara-2, plus Sokil, Shukač, Forecaster, Mini Shark, RQ-35, and Heidrun. Iranian-backed militias launched a fiber-optic FPV drone attack on the US Victory Base near Baghdad International Airport. Ukrainian forces are employing log-periodic directional antennas on "Molniyas" at 3.3 GHz, improving range and resistance to jamming while using vertical/horizontal polarization to counter Russian circular polarization systems. Ukrainian FPV pilots are using feints to dismount Russian troops before striking, and in a first, a Russian soldier with a bayonet charged an FPV drone and was defeated; another Russian soldier was hit by two Ukrainian FPVs while hiding behind a tree.
